data.title: Lilly launches employer-connect platform to broaden weight-loss drug access
data.description:
Eli Lilly said on Thursday it has rolled out a platform to help employers connect with organizations offering low-cost benefits and comprehensive obesity-care programs, aiming to widen access to its blockbuster weight-loss drug Zepbound.

  • Through the Lilly Employer Connect platform, its multi-dose weight-loss drug device Zepbound KwikPen will be available to network pharmacies at a discounted price of $449 for all doses

  • The program provides employees access to weight-loss medicines at lower out-of-pocket costs, and gives employers greater cost predictability and transparency

  • "By enabling coverage outside traditional benefit designs, we lower barriers to treatment and give employers greater control over how they support employee access to obesity care," said Kevin Hern, senior vice president, Lilly Employer

  • The platform enables employers to pair access to Lilly's Zepbound KwikPen with a range of services, including in-person or virtual clinical care and behavior-change support

  • Lilly currently works with more than 15 independent program administrators, including GoodRx , Teladoc Health and Mark Cuban Cost Plus Drug Company, as well as pharmacies across the country, including HealthDyne and CenterWell

  • The program gives employers more flexibility to design a plan that fits their workforce, while giving employees "a clear, trusted path to FDA-approved treatments" that would help curb the potential risks of turning to compounded medications, Teladoc Health executive Kelly Bliss said

  • Lilly and its Danish rival Novo Nordisk have been trying to expand access to their weight-loss treatments and eliminate unapproved, compounded versions, made by combining, mixing or altering drug ingredients

data.title: Distribution Solutions Group Announces 2025 Full Year and Fourth Quarter Results
data.description:
Distribution Solutions Group, Inc., a premier specialty distribution company, has released its consolidated financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ending December 31, 2025. The company reported a 9.8% increase in full-year revenue and generated $84 million in operating cash flow.

Financial Highlights

For the full year 2025, Distribution Solutions Group reported:

  • Revenue of $1.98 billion, up 9.8% from $1.80 billion in 2024.
  • Operating income increased by 39.9% to $78.3 million from $56.0 million in the previous year.
  • Net income of $8.3 million, a significant improvement from a net loss of $7.3 million in 2024.
  • Adjusted EBITDA remained flat at $175.2 million, representing 8.9% of revenue.
  • Cash generated from operations increased to $83.8 million from $56.5 million in 2024.

Business and Operational Highlights

The company experienced growth across its various segments, with notable contributions from five acquisitions partially included in 2024. Organic average daily sales increased by 3.6% compared to the previous year. The company also reported strong cash flow generation, allowing for $23 million in stock repurchases.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

Distribution Solutions Group extended its senior secured credit facility through 2030, providing $700 million in term debt and increasing its revolving credit capacity from $255 million to $400 million. This move enhances the company's financial flexibility to pursue acquisitions and other strategic growth initiatives.

Management's Perspective

CEO and Chairman Bryan King highlighted the company's resilience and strong operating cash flow amidst a challenging macroeconomic environment. He noted that while margins were pressured by end-market softness and other factors, the company is well-positioned for long-term profitable growth.

Future Outlook

Looking ahead to 2026, the company is seeing improved momentum in its weekly sales cadence and is focused on building higher-margin businesses that generate strong free cash flow, aiming to create long-term shareholder value.

Original SEC Filing: Distribution Solutions Group, Inc. [ DSGR ] - 8-K - Mar. 05, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Bioventus Q4 and Full Year 2025 Financial Results
data.description:
Bioventus Inc., a global leader in innovations for active healing, has released its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ending December 31, 2025. The company has also provided its financial guidance for the full year 2026.

Financial Highlights

For the fourth quarter of 2025, Bioventus reported a revenue of $157.9 million, marking a 2.8% increase from the previous year. On an organic basis, revenue grew by 10.0%. The company achieved GAAP earnings of $0.21 per diluted share, a significant improvement from $0.00 in the prior-year period. Non-GAAP earnings were $0.24 per diluted share. Cash from operations for the quarter was $38.0 million, a 97% increase from the previous year.

For the full year 2025, Bioventus reported a revenue of $568.1 million, a slight decrease of 0.9% compared to the prior year, primarily due to the divestiture of the Advanced Rehabilitation Business. On an organic basis, revenue increased by 7.5%. The company reported a net income of $22.7 million, compared to a net loss of $36.1 million in the prior year. Adjusted EBITDA increased by 6.8% to $116.3 million.

Business and Operational Highlights

Bioventus experienced strong demand across its Pain Treatments and Surgical Solutions segments. Pain Treatments revenue grew by 15.1% in Q4, driven by the company's hyaluronic acid therapies for knee osteoarthritis. Surgical Solutions revenue increased by 3.4%, supported by higher U.S. demand for Bone Graft Substitutes. Restorative Therapies revenue was impacted by the divestiture of the Advanced Rehabilitation Business but showed a 10.3% organic growth.

Geographically, U.S. revenue increased by 3.2% in Q4, while international revenue remained flat. However, on an organic basis, international revenue grew by 9.5% due to significant growth in Pain Treatments.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

Bioventus has made significant strides in strengthening its balance sheet by reducing debt by $29 million in Q4. The company also launched its peripheral nerve stimulation products, StimTrial and TalisMann, aimed at providing non-opioid relief from chronic pain. Additionally, Megan Rosengarten joined the executive team as Senior Vice-President and General Manager of Peripheral Nerve Stimulation.

Management's Perspective

Rob Claypoole, President and CEO of Bioventus, expressed satisfaction with the company's performance, highlighting the strong demand for their therapies and disciplined execution across commercial and operational initiatives. He emphasized that the company is entering 2026 from a position of strength, with plans to invest in further accelerating growth, expanding profitability, and generating meaningful cash flow.

Future Outlook

For the full year 2026, Bioventus expects net sales to be between $600 million and $610 million, reflecting a growth of approximately 6% to 7%. The company also anticipates adjusted EPS to be in the range of $0.73 to $0.77, an increase of approximately 7% to 13%. Cash from operations is projected to be between $82 million and $87 million.

Original SEC Filing: Bioventus Inc. [ BVS ] - 8-K - Mar. 05, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: USA Rare Earth to acquire remaining stake in Texas Round Top deposit for $73 million
data.description:
USA Rare Earth said on Thursday it would acquire the remaining minority interest in a Texas rare earth deposit in an all-stock transaction worth $73 million, as part of its efforts to boost domestic production of critical minerals.

The company will acquire all of the outstanding shares of Texas Mineral Resources for about 3.8 million shares of USA Rare Earth common stock.

Shares of USA Rare Earth rose 1% in premarket trading following the news.

Last year, the company had said it will bring forward commercial production at its rare earths project in Texas to late 2028 from 2030, citing faster-than-expected progress at its processing facilities and rising U.S. demand for critical minerals.

The purchase will make USA Rare Earth the sole operator of the Round Top project, which it claims to be the largest known U.S. source of heavy rare earth elements, gallium and beryllium.

Earlier this year, USA Rare Earth said the Trump administration would back a $1.6 billion debt-and-equity funding package to help it build the Texas mine and magnet facility to supply the defense and high-tech sectors.

President Donald Trump had invoked emergency powers last year to boost domestic production of critical minerals as part of a broad effort to offset China's near-total control of the sector.

"This acquisition secures a vital pillar in our strategy to build the world's leading globally integrated, non-China critical mineral technology platform," USA Rare Earth CEO Barbara Humpton said.

The company expects to extract nearly 40,000 metric tons per day of rare earth and critical mineral feedstock by 2030 under its Accelerated Mining Plan.

The transaction, approved by the boards of both companies, is expected to close by the third quarter.

data.title: U.K. Unlikely to Raise Rates in Response to Energy-Price Shock — Market Talk
data.description: The U.K. is likely to slow the pace of rate cuts rather than raise rates in response to higher energy prices, Ruth Gregory at Capital Economics says in a note. While parallels are being made with the jump in energy prices after Russia's invasion of Ukraine, the shock is for now smaller and the economic backdrop very different, she says. Rates remain restrictive, while fiscal policy is being tightened, Gregory says. The Bank of England is now unlikely to cut rates in March, as previously expected. But CE isn't yet changing its rate forecast for this year. "Of course, the situation is highly uncertain and a bigger spike in inflation caused by a larger and more sustained rise in oil and gas prices remains a risk." (don.forbes@wsj.com)

data.title: Invivyd 2025 10-K: Revenue $53.4M, Net Loss $52.5M
data.description:
Invivyd reported fiscal 2025 revenue of $53.4 million driven by product sales after its PEMGARDA emergency use authorization, while reporting a net loss of $52.5 million for the year. The company’s operating loss narrowed to $55.6 million, reflecting improved commercial activity versus 2024. Management highlighted continued commercialization and pipeline progress alongside third‑party manufacturing relationships supporting supply.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue: $53.4 million (2025), up $28.0 million from $25.4 million in 2024, driven by PEMGARDA sales.
  • Gross profit: $49.7 million (implied in filing).
  • Loss from operations (Operating Income): $(55.6) million (2025), improved $121.3 million versus 2024.
  • Net loss (Net Income): $(52.5) million (2025), improved $117.4 million versus 2024.
  • Net loss per share: Metric not provided in the supplied data.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth and commercial launch: Product revenue increased substantially following the FDA emergency use authorization for PEMGARDA in March 2024 and subsequent U.S. commercial sales.
  • Pipeline advancement: VYD2311 moved from nomination to positive Phase 1/2 data and the company initiated the Phase 3 DECLARATION study.
  • Manufacturing and supply: Continued reliance on third‑party CDMOs, with WuXi supplying clinical and commercial product; inventory capitalization began in March 2024 to support commercial supply.
  • Strategic program expansion: New programs nominated including VBY329 for RSV and a measles preclinical target; formation of a SPEAR group focused on Long COVID research.

Original SEC Filing: Invivyd, Inc. [ IVVD ] - 10-K - Mar. 05, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Climb 2025 10-K: $0 Revenue, $(0.88) EPS and $59.9M Net Loss
data.description:
Climb reported zero revenue for the period and a net loss of $59.9 million for 2025, equivalent to a net loss per share of $(0.88). The company’s loss from operations was $(67.9) million in 2025, an improvement vs. the prior year, reflecting increased investment in clinical programs and supporting infrastructure. Management indicates available cash resources expected to fund operations into 2028 while pursuing equity, collaborations or licensing to finance future development.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0 — no revenue from product sales since inception.
  • Operating Income: Loss from operations of $(67.9) million for 2025 (improved $14.1 million vs. 2024).
  • Net Income: Net loss of $(59.9) million for 2025 (improved $14.0 million vs. 2024).
  • Net Income Per Share: Net loss per share, basic and diluted: $(0.88) for 2025.

Business Highlights

  • Pipeline expansion: Acquired budoprutug and licensed CLYM116 in 2024–2025, advancing multiple indications including primary membranous nephropathy (pMN), immune thrombocytopenia (ITP), and systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE), and initiating Phase 1 studies.
  • R&D investment shift: R&D spending increased materially to support clinical and manufacturing activities for budoprutug and CLYM116, reflecting program‑specific development and manufacturing scale‑up.
  • Operational scale‑up: Headcount and general & administrative expenses rose to support growth, including higher legal costs and stock‑based compensation tied to company expansion.
  • Cash runway & financing strategy: Management projects existing cash resources to fund operations into 2028 and is exploring equity financings, collaborations, or licensing arrangements to support further development.

Original SEC Filing: Climb Bio, Inc. [ CLYM ] - 10-K - Mar. 05, 2026

data.title: AMD Shares Rise on Meta AI Deal, Chip Demand
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices (AMD, Financials) shares climbed after the chipmaker announced new artificial intelligence initiatives and a major partnership with Meta Platforms (META, Financials).

The stock rose nearly 6% during trading on March 5 after AMD CEO Lisa Su highlighted strong demand for the company's AI chips and revealed a large-scale collaboration with Meta. The companies are working together on a 6 gigawatt project that will deploy AMD's Instinct GPUs to support Meta's growing AI infrastructure.

Su described the agreement as transformational, reflecting the rapid expansion of AI computing needs among major technology companies.

AMD also announced its new Ryzen AI 400 series CPUs, which are made for laptops and PCs that use AI to power them. This is on top of the Meta alliance. The company claimed that demand for its processors has been more than expected as businesses spend more in AI technologies.

AMD is also putting more money into research and development. The business said it will spend $150 million with Nutanix to build a new AI platform. It also said it would open a research facility at the University of Toronto to work on improving semiconductor technology.

AMD shares ended the day at approximately $202.14, but the price is still down about 9.5% this year because of prior market volatility caused by geopolitical concerns and reductions in the technology industry as a whole.
